channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India Faces Ageing Population Challenges Amid Infrastructure Gaps
item.description: India is ageing rapidly, with the population aged 60 and above projected to more than double from 100 million in 2011 to 230 million by 2036. By last year, elderly people made up 11% of the total popu...

item.title: Tanzania Implements Permit System to Curb Cooking Oil Smuggling
item.description: The Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA) is taking steps to regulate the import of edible oil from Zanzibar to Mainland Tanzania. Commissioner General Yusuph Juma Mwenda announced at a TRA Zanzibar Iftar ...

item.title: Heavy Rain in Karachi Causes 20 Deaths, Multiple Injuries
item.description: KARACHI – A severe thunderstorm hit Karachi on Wednesday night, resulting in 20 deaths and eight injuries, police confirmed on Thursday. The storm brought heavy rain and strong winds that caused multi...

item.title: Iran Launches Missile Attacks on Israel as Tensions Escalate in the Middle East
item.description: GMA Integrated News reported live developments regarding escalating tensions in the Middle East as of March 20, 2026. According to dispatches, a missile fired from Iran landed in a field in the Israel...

item.title: Poilievre comments on US politics; Quebec father and son banned from sperm donation after 613 pregnancies
item.description: On Thursday, March 19, the top stories in Canada included Conservative leader Pierre Poilievre discussing comments by former U.S. President Donald Trump about Canada potentially becoming the '51st sta...

item.title: 'The Price Is Right' Model Holly Hallstrom Details Firing and Legal Battle with Bob Barker
item.description: Holly Hallstrom, a former model on the long-running game show 'The Price Is Right,' has opened up about the circumstances surrounding her firing from the show in 1995. While the official reason given ...

item.title: Australian households and small businesses to see lower electricity costs next financial year
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of Australian households and small businesses are set to benefit from lower electricity prices next financial year under proposed default market offers. The Australian Energy Reg...

item.title: Community and DENR release 70 olive ridley turtles in Zambales to aid conservation
item.description: In a joint effort to support marine wildlife conservation, the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR), together with local community groups, released 70 olive ridley turtle hatchlings ...

item.title: IGP Disu Orders Fresh Promotion Exam To Correct Alleged Corruption in Police Ranks
item.description: Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Tunji Disu don order make senior police officers wey dem no promote during the time of former IGP Kayode Egbetokun do resit promotion exam. According to confidential ...

item.title: Imran Khan’s Sons Allowed to Visit Pakistan on NICOP Cards Without Visa, Says Minister
item.description: Information Minister Attaullah Tarar has confirmed that Imran Khan’s sons, Sulaiman and Kasim, can travel to Pakistan using their National Identity Cards for Overseas Pakistanis (NICOP), which allow t...

item.title: Chuck Norris, 86, dey hospital for Hawaii after small health wahala
item.description: American action movie star and martial artist, Chuck Norris, don land for hospital for Hawaii because of one health issue wey dem never disclose. According to Hollywood news site TMZ, the 86-year-old ...

item.title: Rwanda May Pull Troops from Mozambique Over Funding Concerns
item.description: Rwanda has issued a warning sayin’ if dem no get proper funds, dem go pull out di 1,000 soldiers wey dem deployed in northern Mozambique to fight jihadists. Since 2021, Rwanda troops and police dey he...

item.title: Kanisa la Watawa wa Katoliki liwakaribisha waandishi wa kisiasa kutokaribia maneno ya kuchukia mtu
item.description: Kanisa la Watawa wa Katoliki la Kenya lilipitisha ujumbe wa kwa jambo la usio wa kisiasa lililojikaribisha Kenya kwa kusema kwamba waandishi wa kisiasa wanapaswa kujifunza kuheshimu kila mtu, kuhali k...

item.title: Severe Storms Cause Widespread Flight Disruptions and Tornado Warnings Across Eastern United States
item.description: Severe weather across the eastern United States caused widespread travel disruptions and prompted tornado watches in multiple states on Monday, March 16, 2026. Nearly 60 million people, from northern ...

item.title: Governor Lawal Calls on Muslims to Pray for Peace During Eid El-Fitr
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State don call on all Muslims to increase their prayers for peace as dem dey celebrate Eid El-Fitr. Him talk say this period mark the end of Ramadan, wey na 30 days fas...

item.title: Family continues search for missing Vancouver Island man six years later
item.description: Six years after the disappearance of Michael Gazetas from Courtenay, British Columbia, his family continues to seek answers about what happened to him. Gazetas, then 51, was last seen leaving his home...

item.title: Ministry Urges Learner-Written Plays to Nurture Creativity at Drama Festival
item.description: The Ministry of Education in Kenya is encouraging a shift towards learner-driven performances at this year's Kenya National Drama and Film Festival. Instead of using professionally written scripts, st...

item.title: Iran Confirms Friday as Final Day of Ramadan, Eid to Start Saturday
item.description: Authorities for Iran don yan say Friday go be the last day for the holy month of Ramadan, and Eid al-Fitr celebration go start on Saturday. State TV quote the office of Iran's supreme leader, Mojtaba ...

item.title: U.S. Citizen Sentenced Nearly Three Years for Human Smuggling into Manitoba
item.description: A U.S. citizen, Sahil Aziz, 33, has been sentenced to two years and ten months in prison by a Manitoba provincial court for smuggling a man from Pakistan into Canada near the Emerson border crossing i...

item.title: King Charles III inaugurates Sussex coastal path in his honour
item.description: King Charles III took a walk along the newly opened King Charles III England Coast Path at the Seven Sisters National Nature Reserve in East Sussex, surprising members of the public enjoying a sunny s...
